caption:
Can you call someone your “real friend”?
The answer isn’t always the person who spends the most time with you.
A real friend is someone who helps you become a better person, stands by you when it’s difficult, and tells you the truth even when it’s uncomfortable.
Creator: drtristha
Transcript: Ma'am, how do I know who's our real friend? So I really think a good friend is someone who's by your side no matter what. And they don't judge you and they care about you. And you can be yourself, right? You can share what you have to share. You can cry or you can laugh. You can have a good time and they'll appreciate you for who you are. Ma'am, what to do when someone hurts you in a friendship? If you get hurt by a friend, I think it's important to acknowledge that it happened. Maybe have a conversation with your friend and say, you know what, that hurt me. It's okay to share. But more importantly, you can let go. These things happen. And however old you grow, it will always happen. Because people are different, right? And they come from different places. They have different intentions. And they also might not really realize that they're hurting you in a particular way. So it's okay. You're okay. And you won't stop being kind and compassionate. And you won't stop being a good friend. So that's how you should be. Should I have like two to three friends? Or should I have like a huge group of friends? You should always have those few friends. that know you really, really well and that go on for the rest of your life. And then at every point in life, whether it's primary school, middle school, senior school, when you start working, even at my age, you meet new friends. And not one friend can be all of it. And different friends do different things for you. And everybody should think, hey, Ruhi is my friend. Because what does it mean to be a friend? It's about caring for somebody, helping them. And being a good friend is being a good person, right?

caption:
What makes a great school?
After 15 years of asking that question every single day, we’ve compiled our biggest learnings into one book.
Link In Bio! 
15 Learnings in 15 Years of EKYA is more than a collection of ideas. It’s a practical playbook for educators, school leaders, and anyone committed to building better schools for the next generation.
Creator: drtristha
Transcript: Building schools in India has never been documented. There doesn't exist a playbook on all the successes and failures in learning when someone builds a school system. For this, we have compiled this book, 15 Learnings in 15 Years of Aikia, that talks about everything that we have learned in the last 15 years building a school system. When I started Aikia in 2010, there were a lot of things I did not know. Even though I had a degree from top universities around the world, there were things that I wondered about in how to build a school system. And this book crystallizes all of those learnings from our journals, our post-it notes, our drawings. There are 15 chapters in this book and each covers a section of what makes a school unique. For example, how design shapes learning, how curriculum was built, how the identity of Aikia is embedded, whether it's in the uniform, in the logo, in the campus branding and every touch point for the student. We also covered place of tech in learning and what happens when students go beyond the classroom into the world outside, whether it be outbounds or field trips or internships. It isn't often that schools open out their entire playbook and share with everyone. And this is one such book that we have put together for school leaders, school promoters and people passionate of the education space. space we hope every school benefits from what we have learned and we build a community of school leaders that share and grow the school system for India.

caption:
The best teachers don’t just teach chapters.
They shape confidence, character, and the way students think.
These are three qualities that every great educator carries with them.
Creator: drtristha
Transcript: There are three skills that every great teacher have in common. Number three is that they're always learners first. They're always willing to try something new and learn all the time. Things are changing so rapidly. So if our teachers don't learn every day and come with a learner's mindset, how can they even teach the children? Teachers can focus on what's happening that's new. What can they bring to their classroom that's interesting for their students? They can also learn about child psychology and how children are changing and adapting in this new world. Second, a good teacher prioritizes the child, understands them, puts the effort to know who's in their class and brings the learning to the child. Every child should feel cared for. And once a child feels like this classroom is safe for me, my teacher cares about me, their performance automatically increases. And the most important skill of a teacher is that they believe that every child is unique. To celebrate every achievement of the child and not just be focused on things that she only cares about. Maybe a child creates something, maybe a little card and shows the teacher. It might be a big deal for that child. So a teacher having empathy and understanding, hey, this is my child. This is where my child comes from. And that's special. This is special for this child and celebrating that.
